title: System for producing energy via use of gravity
abstract: The present invention is a system for producing energy via use of gravity and then integrating such a force into a system design of energy power generation by translating the force of gravity into potential energy then into kinetic energy and from kinetic energy back into potential energy again. This system can operate 100 % mechanically or 100% electromechanically by its own power generation, without the need to utilize the aid of an external source of energy or, if required, with the aid of a minimal amount of external energy. A mechanical sequence is introduced in the system's operation to prolong the operational functionality of motion to its working components to the point where full, or substantially full, and independent recycling of fluids takes place and in the process it generates power to run the system's electricity producing generators and supply electricity to the power grid.
claims:
Claims SYSTEM FOR PRODUCING ENERGY VIA USE OF GRAVITY CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . A system for producing energy via use of gravity comprising: a pulley support assembly that supports pulleys and cables that pertain to operation of said system; an upper fluid tank comprising a predetermined amount of fluid and provides a path of control fluid accessibility of said fluid into a plurality of Fluid Transport Cells (FTCs); a lower fluid tank comprising a platform and a predetermined amount of fluid, wherein said platform comprises a plurality of perforators; at least two Fluid Displacement Tanks (FDTs) connecting said upper fluid tank; and said lower fluid tank, wherein each FDT is in operational communication with a corresponding lift assembly of desired mechanical advantage (LADMA) such that each LADMA is responsible for uplift of fluid from said lower fluid tank to said upper fluid tank via said FDTs while the FDT is responsible for reshaping the uplifted fluid to gain potential height such that the uplifted fluid will be ejected into said upper fluid tank, wherein each FDT comprises one between tank door assembly (BTDA), and wherein each LADMA comprises a pulley system of desired mechanical advantage, a door platform assembly, kinetic energy strike platform, and a plurality of cables; at least one motor gear wheel assembly (MGWA), wherein each MGWA comprises at least two motor gear wheels, at least one gear chain, and at least two fluid transport cells (FTCs), wherein said MGWA is connected to at least one electric generator (EG), and wherein each FTC, upon descent, powers a corresponding EG and uplifts the fluid from said lower fluid tank to said upper fluid tank; 

 at least two strike point connecting junctions (SPCJs) hosting a plurality of cables that are to be engaged or, alternatively, disengaged by said corresponding FTC; at least two lift door cones (LDCs) sitting on said platform of said lower fluid tank, wherein each cone lifts inner door of corresponding FTC; and at least two fluid transport cell release platforms (FTCRPs) securing FTCs at potential status until fluid enters into FTCs such that said FTCs are committed to descend. 2. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, further comprising Fluid transport cell emergency shut-off platform (EFTCESOP), at steady state, supports 602. 3. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, further comprising a reverse gear box. 4. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, further comprising a Bee hive dome. 5. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, wherein the descent of the FTCs is a Continuous Descent. 6. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, wherein the descent of the FTCs is a dynamic descent. 7. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, wherein said system operates via a Mechanical sequence. 8. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, wherein said system operates via an Electro-mechanical sequence. 9. The system for producing energy via use of gravity, wherein said system is Modular expanded through the use of a plurality of desired MEPUs.
